concept.shortdesc: Always drive at a speed appropriate for the load and conditions. Slow down in congested

conbody.p: Always drive at a speed appropriate for the load and conditions. Slow down in congested areas, on ramps, and on slippery surfaces.
Carry loads with the forks as low as possible, approximately 200–300 mm above the ground, and with the mast tilted back.
Do not carry loads that obstruct your forward vision. Travel in reverse when the load obstructs forward visibility.
Use the horn at all intersections and blind spots. Yield to pedestrians at all times.
Never carry passengers on the lift truck. The truck is designed for one operator only.
Do not exceed the rated capacity of the lift truck. Check the capacity chart before lifting unusual or awkward loads.
Never travel with a raised load. Lower the load to travel position before moving.
Keep a safe distance from edges, ramps, and overhead obstructions.
Page 54 of 500 | CD100S-7 / CD120S-7 / CD140S-7 / CD160S-7 Operation & Maintenance Manual | DL06P Tier-4
Always follow all safety precautions when operating or maintaining this equipment.
Ensure all operators are properly trained and certified before operating the lift truck.
Regular maintenance is essential to safe and efficient operation of the lift truck.
Contact your local CROWN dealer for parts, service, and technical support.
Keep this manual with the lift truck at all times for reference by operators and technicians.
Record all service and maintenance activities in the maintenance logbook.
Only use genuine CROWN replacement parts to maintain warranty and performance.
